title: Repligen (RGEN) Valuation Check After Mixed Share Price Returns And High P/E Multiple
content:
Never miss an important update on your stock portfolio and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.

Repligen stock snapshot and recent performance

Repligen (RGEN) has drawn investor attention after recent trading left the shares around US$117.78, with the stock showing mixed returns over the past week, month and past 3 months.

See our latest analysis for Repligen.

Recent trading has left Repligen with a 7 day share price return of 6.96%, but a 90 day share price return of 30.49% and a year to date share price return of 28.34%. This may signal fading momentum even though the 1 year total shareholder return is 5.22%.

If you are looking beyond a single stock, this could be a good moment to see what else the market is pricing into 37 healthcare AI stocks

With Repligen trading around US$117.78 and sitting at an estimated 27% discount to one intrinsic value estimate, as well as a wide gap to analyst targets, the key question is whether this signals a mispricing or if markets are already accounting for future growth.

Most Popular Narrative: 37% Undervalued

Repligen's most followed narrative places fair value at about $186.82 versus the last close of $117.78, framing a wide gap that hinges on execution over several years.

Strong and sustained order growth across biopharma, CDMO, and capital equipment segments, supported by record multi-quarter book-to-bill ratios and a robust funnel, positions the company for above-market revenue increases as therapy pipelines expand and demand for advanced bioprocessing solutions rises.

Read the complete narrative.

Want to see what underpins that valuation gap? The narrative leans on rising earnings power, richer margins, and a future earnings multiple that assumes Repligen continues to compound its role in bioprocessing.

Result: Fair Value of $186.82 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, this upbeat narrative could be tested if funding for smaller biotech customers stays weak, or if gene therapy and AAV exposure continues to drag on revenues.

Find out about the key risks to this Repligen narrative.

Another View on Repligen’s Valuation

While the narrative points to Repligen trading at roughly a 27% discount to one intrinsic value estimate, the current P/E of 135.7x is far above the US Life Sciences industry at 37.4x, peers at 36.7x and a fair ratio of 24.6x. This spread raises the risk that expectations are already very demanding and prompts the question of where the market might eventually settle.
